message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,365 reviews; OpenTable ~4,220 reviews (4.7); Tripadvisor 142 (3.9, #88/281)
- Sentiment: mostly positive overall (strong on OpenTable/Yelp), mixed on Tripadvisor
- Longevity: Opened 2018; ~8 years operating; stable single-owner (Onural Group). Chef Onural has operated Coachella Valley restaurants continuously since 2011 (The Venue, Palm Desert).
- Credentials: ["Chef Engin Onural — Japanese-government-designated sushi ambassador","Chef Engin Onural — 27th place, World Sushi Cup/Olympics 2017 (US representative)","Best Chefs America index placement (chef-level)","Palm Springs Life '40 Under 40' honoree, 2013 (chef-level)","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice designation","Routine inclusion in regional 'best sushi' editorial lists (Visit Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life Asian-restaurants list)","Featured at Palm Desert Food & Wine Festival [UNVERIFIED specific years]"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces consistently for: 'best sushi Palm Springs', 'best sushi Coachella Valley', 'date night Palm Springs sushi', 'Japanese whiskey bar Palm Springs', 'uptown Palm Springs dining'. Does NOT dominate broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' queries (the latter belongs to Nobu Indian Wells, Mikado JW Marriott, and El Paseo concepts).
- Site structured data: Own site (sandfishsushiwhiskey.com) presents clean address, phone, email, and hours in HTML text but does NOT carry visible Schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup. Menus are linked out to Toast and Postmates rather than embedded as structured data. Agent-readability is moderate: legible to text-extracting agents but missing semantic restaurant schema that would enable rich answers.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"5 Questions with Sushi Olympian and Sandfish Chef-Owner Engin Onural - PHOENIX magazine","url":"https://www.phoenixmag.com/2024/09/04/5-questions-with-sushi-olympian-and-sandfish-chef-owner-engin-onural/"},{"title":"About | Sandfish Sushi and Whiskey","url":"https://www.sandfishsushiwhiskey.com/about"},{"title":"A Symphony of Flavors: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey provides an ‘unforgettable culinary experience’ | Dining | phoenix.org","url":"https://www.phoenix.org/lovin_life/dining/a-symphony-of-flavors-sandfish-sushi-whiskey-provides-an-unforgettable-culinary-experience/article_cc7d87c4-92be-11ee-972c-4ff82b8cf989.html"},{"title":"Engin Onural - Award Winning Sushi Chef","url":"https://www.enginonural.com/"},{"title":"Introducing Sushi Chef Engin Onural, Our Newest Best Chef | Best Chefs America","url":"https://www.bestchefsamerica.com/engin-onural-best-chefs-feature-palm-desert-sushi-about"},{"title":"List of Senior Officials | Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan","url":"https://www.mofa.go.jp/about/hq/list2.html"},{"title":"The Venue's Owner Takes Step Toward Master Sushi Chef","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-venues-owner-takes-step-toward-master-sushi-chef/"},{"title":"Success Stories of Graduates - Sushi Chef Institute","url":"https://sushischool.net/success-stories-of-graduates/"},{"title":"Japan's Ambassadors, Consuls, and Representatives Abroad","url":"https://embassies.info/japan/ambassadors"},{"title":"The List of Japanese Cuisine Goodwill Ambassadors ( ...","url":"https://www.maff.go.jp/e/policies/market/attach/pdf/ambassa-6.pdf"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Engin Onural and his designation as a sushi ambassador:

Engin Onural is among a small group of sushi ambassadors designated by the Japanese government. 

More specifically, in 2013, Onural took a test to become accredited through a formal system offered by the Japanese government, and he passed, becoming one of the few people in his region to be honored by the All Japanese Sushi Association with a Sushi Proficiency Certificate.

He walked into the classroom with 10 years of training, the only English speaker present, and walked out the only Turkish man alive to hold the certificate.

Beyond this designation, he reached 27th place at the Sushi World Cup in 2017 and was honored with the title of Best Chef America in 2016.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about "The Venue" sushi restaurant in Palm Desert:

In 2011, Engin opened The Venue Sushi Bar & Sake Lounge in Palm Desert, California — garnering accolades for both his restaurant and himself. The restaurant is owned and operated by owner/chef Engin Onural.

Every year since, Palm Springs Life Magazine has voted The Venue as the "Best Sushi in Coachella Valley." The restaurant is located at 73-111 El Paseo Ste: 103 Palm Desert, Ca 92260 and is open everyday from 4:30pm–9pm with happy hour (daily) 4:30pm–6:00pm.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey in Palm Springs:

Sandfish Sushi and Whiskey has been serving modern sushi delicacies in uptown Palm Springs since 2018. Chef and founder Engin Onural draws in cultural influences from his world travels to create a truly unique dining experience.

The restaurant is operated by Onural Group. Chef Engin Onural was born in New York, raised in Turkey, and has called the Coachella Valley home since 2006. He opened his first restaurant, The Venue Sushi & Sake Bar in Palm Desert in 2011 and Sandfish Sushi and Whiskey in Palm Springs in 2018.

The restaurant's concept brings together the refined art of sushi and the robust character of whiskey, creating an experience that feels both innovative and grounded in tradition. Chef Engin Onural is among a small group of sushi ambassadors designated by the Japanese government, and in 2017 he placed 27th in the World Sushi Olympics.

content.content:
# Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey - Review Metrics

**Review Count:** 142 reviews

**Rating:** 3.9 of 5 bubbles

**Ranking:** "#88 of 281 Restaurants in Palm Springs"

**Travelers' Choice Award:** No badge is displayed for this restaurant. The page shows what a Travelers' Choice award represents ("consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties"), but Sandfish does not have this designation.

# Engin Onural's Awards and Credentials

Based on the webpage content, here are the recognized awards and honors listed for Chef Engin Onural:

**Certifications & Official Recognition:**
- Japanese government sushi certification
- Certified sake sommelier
- Spot on the Best Chefs America Index

**Competitive Achievement:**
- Representative of America in the World Sushi Cup

**Magazine & Industry Recognition:**
- Featured on Palm Springs Life Magazine's "40 Under 40" list (2013) — described as young influencers in Coachella Valley
- Annual participant in celebrity chef demonstrations at the Palm Desert Food & Wine Festival
- Recurring feature in Palm Springs Life Magazine, which voted The Venue as "Best Sushi in Coachella Valley" annually

**Media Accolades:**
- Featured in numerous publications including Forbes, Hollywood Reporter, Eater LA, and Timeout

The webpage emphasizes his professional credentials and industry standing rather than listing specific award names or competition placements beyond his World Sushi Cup representation.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Engin Onural and the Best Chefs America Index:

Engin Onural was the first Best Chef to be added to the Best Chefs America index for the year of 2015. However, some sources reference this recognition as occurring in 2016. He was named Best Chef America in 2016, indicating that while he was added to the 2015 index, the public announcement or recognition may have been in 2016.

The 31-year-old sushi chef is viewed by his peers as a prodigious sushi craftsman, accomplished beyond his years. Before earning his sushi certification from the Japanese government, securing his place on the Best Chefs America Index, and representing America in the World Sushi Cup, Chef Engin Onural was a 10-year old with a dream.

Based on the search results, I found information about Sandfish in Palm Springs:

Sandfish is located at 1556 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262, and has 1815 photos and 1365 reviews on Yelp. 

It's an award-winning sushi restaurant by Chef Engin Onural, established in 2018. The restaurant's hours are Monday-Thursday 4:30 pm - 10:00 pm, Friday-Saturday 11:00 am - 2:30 pm and 4:30 pm - 11:00 pm, and Sunday 11:00 am - 2:30 pm and 4:30 pm - 10:00 pm.

The restaurant is known for its sushi and whiskey offerings. Since Covid, the dress code has been relaxed, and as long as you're not wearing a bathing suit, you're good to go.

content.text: I have enough verification. Submitting the structured output.

confirms: Opened 2018 in uptown Palm Springs — confirmed by multiple sources (sandfishsushiwhiskey.com, palmsprings.com, Onural Group site)
Operated by Onural Group (single-owner, chef-owner Engin Onural) — confirmed
Chef Engin Onural opened The Venue Sushi Bar & Sake Lounge in Palm Desert in 2011 — confirmed by enginonural.com, Onural Group, and 2011-era press
World Sushi Cup 2017 27th place / US representative — confirmed by Phoenix Magazine, sandfishsushiwhiskey.com, and chef's own site

Japanese-government sushi certification (often described as 'sushi ambassador') — confirmed via Palm Springs Life ('The Venue's Owner Takes Step Toward Master Sushi Chef'), Phoenix Magazine, and chef site; multiple sources describe a Japanese-government-issued Sushi Proficiency Certificate
Best Chefs America index placement — confirmed (chef has profile pages on bestchefsamerica.com; press coverage 2015-2016)
Palm Springs Life '40 Under 40' honoree, 2013 — confirmed via enginonural.com biography
Routine inclusion in regional 'best sushi' editorial lists — confirmed (Palm Springs Life repeated 'Best Sushi in Coachella Valley')
Yelp 1,365 reviews — confirmed (Yelp page header shows 1815 photos & 1365 reviews)
Tripadvisor 142 reviews / 3.9 rating / #88 of 281 in Palm Springs — confirmed by direct fetch
Featured at Palm Desert Food & Wine Festival — confirmed as recurring participant (Palm Springs Life coverage of demonstrations), though specific years remain unverified
contradicts: 'Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice designation' — REFUTED. Direct fetch of the Sandfish Tripadvisor page shows NO Travelers' Choice award badge. The restaurant ranks #88 of 281 (well outside the top 10% threshold required for the award) with a 3.9 rating. The claim should be removed from credentials.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable ~4,220 reviews at 4.7 — not independently confirmed in this pass (OpenTable page surfaced but exact count/rating not directly fetched)

'Japanese-government-designated sushi ambassador' wording — chef holds a Japanese-government Sushi Proficiency Certificate / sushi certification, but the formal 'Goodwill Ambassador' designation listed by Japan's MAFF could not be independently confirmed (MAFF PDF returned 403). The 'ambassador' framing may be marketing language for the certification rather than a formal MAFF Goodwill Ambassador appointment — recommend softening to 'Japanese-government sushi-certified chef'
Palm Desert Food & Wine Festival specific years of appearance — confirmed as a recurring participant but exact years still unverified
Chef Onural 'continuously since 2011' operating Coachella Valley restaurants — The Venue opened 2011 and still operates per current website; presumed continuous but no gap-check performed
input.notes: Core history (2018 opening, 2011 Venue origin, single-owner Onural Group) holds up well. Chef-level credentials (World Sushi Cup 2017, Best Chefs America, 40 Under 40 2013, Japanese sushi certification) all corroborated by multiple independent sources. Two concerns: (1) the 'Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice' claim is demonstrably wrong — no badge present, ranking #88/281 is far below the top-10% threshold — this should be removed. (2) The 'Japanese-government-designated sushi ambassador' phrasing risks overstating the credential; primary sources describe a Japanese-government Sushi Proficiency Certificate / certification rather than the formal MAFF Goodwill Ambassador designation (MAFF list could not be fetched for direct verification). No James Beard or Michelin claims were made (correctly — none would appear).
